title: Wavy Australian Salmon Casserole
description: Briny, flaky, energizing Australian dish featuring sashimi-cut salmon and a creamy dill topping.
instructions: Lightly oil a 2 liter dutch oven with a tablespoon of olive oil.
Season the sashimi cut salmon fillets with 1 tsp salt and 0.5 tsp black pepper.
Place the salmon in the oven and bake for 12 minutes until the edges are opaque.
While salmon cooks, melt 200g cream cheese with 100g Greek yogurt in a small saucepan over low heat.
Stir in 2 tbsp chopped dill, 1 tsp lemon zest, 1 tbsp capers, and 1 small diced shallot into the cheese mixture.
When the salmon is nearly done, stir in 150g fresh spinach until wilted, then mix in 150g cherry tomatoes.
Remove the salmon from the oven and spread the cheese mixture evenly over the fillets.
Return the casserole to the oven and bake for an additional 8 minutes until the top turns golden.
Let rest 5 minutes, then garnish with extra dill before serving.
